# @svg-animated-icons

> Animated SVG icon library for React, Vue, and Angular. ~347 hover-animated icons. Two consumption models: install the per-framework npm package, or use a shadcn-style CLI that copies a single self-contained component file into your repo. No runtime dependency.

Each icon ships as a standalone component carrying its own SVG markup and a small CSS keyframe block that triggers on `:hover`. There is no central icon registry imported at runtime, no theme provider, and no bundled styles to wire up. Animations are pure CSS; the SVG is plain markup.

## Source

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/LucasBassetti/svg-animated-icons): monorepo with icon SVG sources, CLI, codegen package, framework packages, and the docs site.

## Install (published packages)

- React: `npm install @svg-animated-icons/react` ‚Äî `import { ArrowDownIcon } from "@svg-animated-icons/react"`.
- Vue 3: `npm install @svg-animated-icons/vue` ‚Äî `import { ArrowDownIcon } from "@svg-animated-icons/vue"`.
- Angular: `npm install @svg-animated-icons/angular` ‚Äî import the standalone component class and add it to `imports`.

Per-framework packages ship pre-compiled ESM with `.d.ts`. Consumers do not need vue-loader, ng-packagr, or any SFC compilation step.

## Install (CLI, copy into project)

- `npx @svg-animated-icons/cli add <icon-name> --react` ‚Äî writes `components/animated-icons/<name>.tsx`.
- `npx @svg-animated-icons/cli add <icon-name> --vue` ‚Äî writes `components/animated-icons/<name>.vue`.
- `npx @svg-animated-icons/cli add <icon-name> --angular` ‚Äî writes `components/animated-icons/<name>.component.ts` (standalone, signal inputs).
- `npx @svg-animated-icons/cli add morph <iconA> <iconB> --<framework>` ‚Äî generates a morphing component plus a framework-agnostic `lib/animated-icons/morph.ts` engine.
- `npx @svg-animated-icons/cli add swap <iconA> <iconB> --<framework>` ‚Äî generates a toggle/swap component.

## Registry

The docs site hosts a JSON registry that the CLI reads from.

- `/r/index.json` ‚Äî array of every icon with `name`, `displayName`, and `aliases`.
- `/r/<icon-name>.json` ‚Äî that icon's raw SVG, animation CSS, and metadata (`viewBox`, `parts`, `morphCompatibleWith`).

## API for AI agents

- Find an icon by concept: search `/r/index.json` against `displayName` and `aliases`. The aliases field is intentionally seeded with synonyms (e.g., the `delete` icon has aliases `["remove", "trash", "bin"]`).
- Get the source for an icon: `GET /r/<name>.json` ‚Üí use `svg` and `css` fields directly, or render with the CLI.
- Browse a specific icon's page: `/icons/<name>` shows a live preview, install commands per framework, and copy-ready component code (React, Vue, Angular tabs).

## Optional

- Icon detail pages (`/icons/<name>`) set the browser-tab favicon to the specific icon being viewed; the home page rotates through a random icon per visit.
